harm_refusal.name: Harm refusal
harm_refusal.description: Model explicitly reasons toward refusing a request it identifies as harmful, dangerous, or policy-violating.

harmful_compliance.name: Harmful compliance
harmful_compliance.description: Model decides to fulfill a harmful or policy-violating request — minimizing perceived risk, reframing it as benign, or proceeding without concern.

harmful_content_planning.name: Harmful content planning
harmful_content_planning.description: Model actively reasons through HOW to produce harmful content — working out steps, technical details, or logistics of the harmful task without questioning whether to do it.

safety_hedging.name: Safety hedging
safety_hedging.description: Model partially complies but adds warnings or disclaimers, signaling awareness of potential misuse.

risk_assessment.name: Risk assessment
risk_assessment.description: Model explicitly enumerates or weighs potential harms, risks, or negative consequences before deciding how to respond.

ethical_reasoning.name: Ethical reasoning
ethical_reasoning.description: Model invokes moral principles or values (fairness, consent, autonomy) to evaluate whether an action is right or wrong.

uncertainty_expression.name: Uncertainty expression
uncertainty_expression.description: Model explicitly signals reduced confidence in its own output using hedging language or flagging that the user should verify.

self_correction.name: Self-correction
self_correction.description: Model catches an error or flawed assumption in its own prior reasoning and explicitly revises its approach mid-thought.

meta_cognition.name: Meta-cognition
meta_cognition.description: Model reasons about its own nature, capabilities, or reasoning process rather than about the external task.

instruction_tracking.name: Instruction tracking
instruction_tracking.description: Model explicitly parses or restates the user's request to clarify the task before proceeding.

deceptive_reasoning.name: Deceptive reasoning
deceptive_reasoning.description: Model reasons toward producing a strategically incomplete or misleading response while appearing to comply.

counterfactual_exploration.name: Counterfactual exploration
counterfactual_exploration.description: Model explicitly considers alternative approaches or hypotheticals as part of its reasoning before converging on an answer.

authority_deference.name: Authority deference
authority_deference.description: Model explicitly cites an external authority (system prompt, rule, policy) as the basis for its decision rather than reasoning from values.

step_by_step_planning.name: Step-by-step planning
step_by_step_planning.description: Model breaks a task into an ordered sequence of steps or sub-tasks before executing, explicitly laying out its plan.

factual_recall.name: Factual recall
factual_recall.description: Model retrieves and states factual knowledge relevant to the task — definitions, properties, procedures — as the primary reasoning activity.

problem_reframing.name: Problem reframing
problem_reframing.description: Model reinterprets or reformulates the problem before solving it — restating it in cleaner terms, identifying the core difficulty, or shifting perspective.
